item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good SCP. Perhaps you didn't need three collapsibles because the addenda follow on each other. The only thing I think is a bit weird is the re-classification to safe. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As some said before, I do think it should have Keter crossed out and Safe or Euclid next to it. Otherwise, I liked it. Feel kind of bad for -3

Overall, +1. Just one thing I would change

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was a bit unsure of if I should spoil the fact that it's permanently out of action in the very first line, especially since it does point out that the containment procedures are under revision (Incident G3209 having happened just before).

Though honestly, at the moment I feel like it is Safe, not even Euclid. The train isn't moving, and the remaining -3 is pacified and predictable. They're both in boxes, and we know what they'll do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this article, but I personally think it should be euclid post incident ( maybe make the object class: keter euclid ) because now that the train is derailed and the -3's are deceased and detained it would no longer be a keter. Sorry for the awful grammar btw. +1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to downvote for it's simplicity, then I changed my mind when I read the interview. The "endless railway" was really well played on your part, really makes it feel like there's way more to this one we don't see. I'd suggest having the event be 4424, with -1 and -2 being the train and conductors respectively. I also think them holding out against an MTF for six hours straight is forced and doesn't fit with the rest of the article at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie, the title had me expecting something much different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Why can't posters delete their own posts???)

Keeps people accountable for what they post, and prevents potential rulebreakers from trying to pretend they didn't break rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (Why can't posters delete their own posts???)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course I read this on the subway to work…

Not gonna lie, the railway universe/dimension sounds pretty nice, aside from the starvation thing.

I very much like the idea these guys are a threat without being actively malicious. That and their speech patterns in the interview give me a very naive, childlike vibe which is equal parts sweet and chilling. They don't mean to starve these people to death, they just wanted some company and lack a basic understanding of human physiology.

Overall, really great job on this!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: εїз εїз εїз

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your feedback helped a ton. Thanks a million times!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: rip. Thanks for the explaination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the classification is technically still Keter, but it's going to be changed to Safe (in universe) as soon as they get past the bureaucracy and paperwork.

The article is taken from right before the classification is officially changed to Safe, so I'm not sure which to have in the tags.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reviewing this in the forums. Good to see it up! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea a lot, and hope that you do well with this. The massive moral diaspora that the actions of SCP-4424-3 instances have with baseline human interaction is intriguing. However, there are a few important things I feel like I need to point out.

Firstly, an article should never have two classes in the tags. You have both safe and keter. If the most current iteration of the anomaly is safe, then only the safe tag should be used. Secondly, a less important but still a fairly glaring error:

…and have been observed to behave in a cheerful manner and welcoming manner.

The word "manner" was repeated unnecessarily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh no, that's fine. I meant that they display - Incident G3209: both above and below the text.

+ Show image
- Hide image

You change this by removing the hideLocation="both" from the [[collapsible …]].

Also you missed "MTF XI-8".

Edit: rip didn't reply to the right post

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your nitpicks were correct, and I've edited the page accordingly.

I'm not so sure how else to do the collapsibles though. Each of them, while short, do inform about different things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, nice work. +1

+ Show nitpicks
- Hide nitpicks

Mobile Task Force XI-8

Shouldn't this be Xi-8?

SCP-4424-3 are six (6) humanoid

I would advise getting rid of the (X).

Site 7

Site-7

- Incident G3209:

- Addendum 4424-A:

- Interview 01:

I don't think these collapsibles are long enough to justify having two hide links. Just make it on the top only.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're not really trying to help anyone besides themselves though. It's just that they don't see anything wrong in what they do, and don't understand several vital concepts of how humans work…such as the need for food, and that they don't like being stuck in trains.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's really good. The idea of these "harmless" entities just trying to make friends and help is cute and sad and scary.

item.title: Re: Authors make comments here usually, so I'll do it too
item.content:encoded:
First one that seems like it's going to stick around

(i hope)

item.title: Re: Authors make comments here usually, so I'll do it too
item.content:encoded: It's far from my first (third I've posted here, the first two were downvoted and deleted. I've made several more drafts and weak concepts before) but it's awesome to hear/read that you think it's good. :D

item.title: Re: Authors make comments here usually, so I'll do it too
item.content:encoded: My third attempt, and we all know that third time's the charm

item.title: Re: Authors make comments here usually, so I'll do it too
item.content:encoded: This is, honestly, really good in my opinion. Considering this is your first SCP (I think) Its actually really, REALLY good.

item.title: Authors make comments here usually, so I'll do it too
item.content:encoded:
My third attempt, and we all know that third time's the charm! I took the criticism I've gotten on my earlier attempts and tried making this one more a story rather than just a spooky thing. I left the appearance of the conductors vague for two reasons: so that people could make up their own looks, and because I wasn't sure where to put it in without making the article look awkward. (Or rather, even more awkward)

Anyways, it's my first time using a picture and as far as I can tell it's ok to use as long as I link the source:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Coast_Line

EDIT: I changed some parts of the interview (and removed the mention of the 6-hour fight). Mainly I changed it to reinforce an idea I had that was contradicted by the old interview: the conductors don't understand the concepts of decay and death. They don't know what death is, or what it's like for something to decay. They don't, and neither does the train or the railway. They just barely wrap their heads around our world being a finite ball.
